1. Act as ONE

Chapter 2

Code of Conduct

1. Act As ONE

Responsibility & General Standards

The Code of Conduct is a collective commitment. While leadership sets the tone, every employee at ONE is responsible for maintaining our unified and safe culture. This section explores the specific roles leadership and employees play in safeguarding our values.

Click on each role to see responsibility and general standards of behaviour at ONE:

Top Management
ONE Employees

KNOWLEDGE CHECK

Bad Example

It is the end of the financial year, and Sarah has a backlog of payments to process. She feels frustrated that each payment requires approval, as it is inconvenient and slows her down. She decides to bypass the approval process to clear her backlog.

CONTINUE

section_1.top_management_excerpt:
Top Management

Tone from the Top: Top management acts with integrity, showing their commitment to achieving the spirit and ideals set forth in this Code.

Enforcement: Take any violations of this Code seriously, hold any ONE employees who do not comply with this Code accountable, regardless of position.

Accountability: Accountable not only for the actions of ONE employees but also for those of our business partners.

Employees do what leaders do, not just what leaders say.

ONE Employees

Act ethically and professionally in every business function, prioritising fairness and respect.

Foster a professional environment built on honesty and mutual respect, with zero tolerance for discrimination, harassment, violence or unethical behaviour.

Uphold ONE’s core values and Business Credo by acting with social and ethical responsibility in everything you do.

KNOWLEDGE CHECK

2. Safeguard our Environment, Safety and Quality

Chapter 2

Code of Conduct

2. Safeguard our Environment, Safety and Quality

Our People, Planet and Partners

Our success is built on more than just operational efficiency; it is rooted in a steadfast commitment to our people, our planet, and our partners. By adhering to these standards, we strive not only to meet legal requirements but also to set the benchmark for excellence in the global maritime industry.

Click on each tab to see our commitment:

PURSUIT OF SAFETY
SAFEGUARDING OUR ENVIRONMENT
IMPROVEMENT OF SERVICE QUALITY

We ensure safety in all our operations.

We comply with all applicable health and safety laws and regulations in the regions where we operate.

We contribute to ongoing improvements in transport safety technologies to enhance ONE's pursuit of safety efforts and remain actively engaged in safety-related know-how development.

We protect the environment and ecosystems by taking action on climate change, preventing marine pollution and using resources responsibly.

3. Observe Ethical Conduct and Compliance with Laws and Regulations

Chapter 2

Code of Conduct

3. Observe Ethical Conduct and Compliance with Laws and Regulations

Ethical Business Conduct

Beyond mere adherence, ONE champions a culture where governance fuels value. We hold ourselves to the highest standards of integrity and transparency, ensuring excellence is built into the fabric of our global operations.

Comply with Laws, Regulations, and ONE Policies

Numbered divider
1

The Basics

At ONE, compliance is not just about avoiding a penalty; it is about doing the right thing even when no one is looking. You are expected to understand and follow the laws, rules, and guidelines applicable to your role.

Numbered divider
2

Ensure Fair Competition

Shipping is a global, interconnected business. To keep the industry healthy, we must ensure a level playing field. Anti-Trust laws are designed to prevent monopolies and unfair advantages.

You are strictly prohibited from engaging in any activity that restricts fair and open competition. Click on each flip-card below to learn about prohibited activities:

Click to flip
Click to flip

Agreeing on rates with competitors.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Dividing up territories or customers with other lines.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Secretly coordinating on tenders or contracts.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Misusing your position at ONE to force favourable terms.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Sharing sensitive information or non-public information with competitors.

Numbered divider
3

Prohibiting Bribery & Corruption

At ONE, there is no "grey area" when it comes to bribery. We do not offer, give, or accept bribes
section_3.indirect_trap_excerpt:
The "Indirect" Trap

You are responsible for the actions of third parties acting on ONE’s behalf. Using an agent, broker, or consultant to "handle" a bribe does not protect you; it makes you a participant in the crime.

High-Risk Interactions

Government Officials: Interactions with government officials like port authorities, customs officers, and maritime regulators require extreme caution.

Numbered divider
4

Gifts, Hospitality, and Entertainment

While building business relationships is important, the line between a "gesture of goodwill" and an "attempt to inappropriately influence" must never be blurred.

Classify the following actions according to whether they are a Gesture of Goodwill or could be seen as an Attempt to Inappropriately Influence?

Drag them to the correct classification.

Current item: Offering ONE-branded calendars to port agents during New Year visits.
Offering ONE-branded calendars to port agents during New Year visits.
Gifting a luxury watch to a Terminal Manager to secure a priority berth.
Accepting a vendor's notebook and calendar at a Shipping conference.
A vendor offers your son an internship during contract negotiations.
Sending a fruit basket to a long-term partner during the holidays.
Covering "travel expenses" for a Ministry official's family vacation.
A headhunter offers you a personal financial incentive to hire their candidate.
Client giving

5

Selection of Suppliers

At ONE, we select partners based on merit, not "who we know."

When selecting suppliers:
section_3.stepper_excerpt:
1 of 4
Managing conflict

Managing conflict is not just about avoiding bad behaviour; it is about a proactive process.

START
2 of 4
Step 1
1. Identify

Ask yourself: Could this situation affect—or appear to affect—my ability to act in ONE's best interests?

3 of 4
Step 2
2. Disclose

Promptly declare any potential conflict to your Head of Department, HR, or Legal/Compliance.

4 of 4
Step 3
3. Manage

Work with your Head of Department to create a mitigation plan (e.g., exclude yourself from the project).

Numbered divider
7

Fraud Prevention and Anti-Money Laundering

Fraud and money laundering are not just "financial crimes"—they are threats to our reputation, our stability, and our community.

ONE adopts a zero-tolerance approach to any fraud or attempted fraudulent practices.

Your Role: The Three Pillars

Click on each pillar to reveal your role in Fraud Prevention and Anti-Money Laundering:

Click to flip
Click to flip

Never participate in or help someone else carry out a fraudulent act.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Turning a blind eye is a violation of policy. If you see something, you are responsible for reporting it.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Maintain a "culture of awareness." Be the person who notices when something doesn't add up.

Identify the threat

Drag the correct Fraud Category card from the left and drop it onto the corresponding scenario on the right.

F
section_3.q1_initial_excerpt:
Q.1

Scenario: The Weekend "Consultant" (Secondary Employment)

David, a Senior Analyst in the Finance department, is also a highly skilled coder. On weekends, he works as a paid freelance software developer for a technology firm. David believes that the freelance job does not conflict with his main job at ONE.

He decides he does not need to notify ONE. Is David's assessment correct?

Choose the best answer

A. Yes. Since David does this on his own time and it is not a finance role, there is no conflict.

B. No. Even a perceived conflict of interest must be managed. David has skipped the "Identify and Disclose" steps of his responsibility.

C. Yes. As long as David does not use ONE’s information or laptop, he is free to work elsewhere on weekends.

D. No. He is not allowed to have any second job, regardless of the industry, because ONE employees must dedicate 100% of their energy to the Company.

SUBMIT


Incorrect
TAKE AGAIN

Q.2

Scenario: Agree or Walk Away?

You are at a Shipping Association meeting where several competitors are present. During a coffee break, a representative from another carrier says: "The port congestion in this region is getting expensive. We should all agree to a standard 'Congestion Fee' of $200 per TEU to recover our costs. It is better for everyone if we are on the same page, right?"

What should you do?

Choose the best answer

A. Agree verbally:
section_3.q2_initial_excerpt:
Q.2

Scenario: Agree or Walk Away?

You are at a Shipping Association meeting where several competitors are present. During a coffee break, a representative from another carrier says: "The port congestion in this region is getting expensive. We should all agree to a standard 'Congestion Fee' of $200 per TEU to recover our costs. It is better for everyone if we are on the same page, right?"

What should you do?

Choose the best answer

A. Agree verbally: It makes sense for the industry to have a standard fee.

B. Stay silent and take notes: Do not say anything, but bring the info back to your team.

C. Object and Walk Out: Clearly state: "ONE does not discuss or coordinate pricing or surcharges with competitors." Immediately leave the room and ensure your departure is noted.

SUBMIT


Incorrect

Sharing and exchanging price information is illegal. By staying in the room, you could be seen as a "silent conspirator" in a price-fixing cartel.

TAKE AGAIN

4. Nurture an Inclusive Workplace and Respect for Human Rights

Chapter 2

Code of Conduct

4. Nurture an Inclusive Workplace and Respect for Human Rights

At ONE, we create and support a diverse and inclusive workplace with equal opportunities. Respecting human and labour rights across ONE's operations and zero-tolerance for all forms of child labour, forced labour or other forms of exploitation.

HERE IS HOW WE DO IT

1. Diversity, Inclusion, and Non-Discrimination

Click on each tab to learn more:

WE BUILD THE HOUSE
OUR PEOPLE MAKE IT HOME

We view diversity as both a moral responsibility and a key driver of sustainable growth. By embracing a wide range of human resources, we generate organisational vitality and innovation. Diversity at ONE goes beyond what we see. It includes: Personality, race, gender, ways of thinking, values, perception, and professional experiences.

We are dedicated to providing an inclusive workplace culture and environment. We strictly prohibit discrimination based on:

Age, pregnancy, caregiving duties, marital status, gender, race, nationality, religion, language, physical disability, mental health conditions or any other characteristic protected under local laws and regulations.

We proactively encourage an environment where every voice is heard and every person can contribute their best work.

We ensure equal opportunity in all aspects of employment.

2. We Guarantee a Safe and Healthy Work Environment

Click on each card to learn more about our commitment to ONE work environment:

Click to flip
Click to flip

We actively support an environment that balances work with private life.

Click to flip
Click to flip

section_4.grammar_excerpt:
Our team connect with the students in Vietnam to donate and support for future generations

KNOWLEDGE CHECK

Q.1

Scenario: The "Funny" Meme

A team member posts a meme in the ONE Workvivo group that makes a joke about a specific bank holiday. While most of the team reacts with "laugh" emojis, you notice one colleague, who celebrates that holiday, goes quiet and withdraws from the chat.

Which of the following statements are TRUE according to the ONE Code of Conduct?

Choose all the correct answers (Select three correct answers)

A. This is considered harassment because it creates an uncomfortable or offensive environment, even if it was intended as a joke.

B. It is acceptable behaviour because the majority of the team laughed, so the meme helps build team spirit.

C. This violates the Code because Workvivo is a work-related platform, and professional standards apply there just like in the office.

D. You should speak up or report the post, because "Acting as ONE" means ensuring no one feels excluded or disrespected.

E. It is only a violation if the colleague who went quiet files a formal written complaint with the HR department.

SUBMIT


Incorrect

ONE does not tolerate harassment in any form, whether verbal, physical, visual, or digital. This covers conduct in virtual environments and work-related messaging platforms. If you face such a situation or are aware of such a sit
section_4.q1_initial_excerpt:
Q.1

Scenario: The "Funny" Meme

A team member posts a meme in the ONE Workvivo group that makes a joke about a specific bank holiday. While most of the team reacts with "laugh" emojis, you notice one colleague, who celebrates that holiday, goes quiet and withdraws from the chat.

Which of the following statements are TRUE according to the ONE Code of Conduct?

Choose all the correct answers (Select three correct answers)

A. This is considered harassment because it creates an uncomfortable or offensive environment, even if it was intended as a joke.

B. It is acceptable behaviour because the majority of the team laughed, so the meme helps build team spirit.

C. This violates the Code because Workvivo is a work-related platform, and professional standards apply there just like in the office.

D. You should speak up or report the post, because "Acting as ONE" means ensuring no one feels excluded or disrespected.

E. It is only a violation if the colleague who went quiet files a formal written complaint with the HR department.

SUBMIT


Incorrect

ONE does not tolerate harassment in any form, whether verbal, physical, visual, or digital. This covers conduct in virtual environments and work-related messaging platforms. If you face such a situation or are aware of such a situation happening in ONE, please Speak Up and escalate.

TAKE AGAIN

Q.2

Scenario: The Campaign Flyer

A coll
section_4.q2_initial_excerpt:
Q.2

Scenario: The Campaign Flyer

A colleague asks to leave a stack of flyers for their favourite political candidate in the office breakroom during an election season.

Can you grant this request?

Choose the best answers 

A. Yes, as long as they do not force anyone to take one.

B. Yes, provided that colleagues supporting other candidates are also allowed to leave their flyers.

C. No

D. No, unless the candidate specifically supports the maritime industry.

SUBMIT


Incorrect

ONE maintains political neutrality. ONE Employees are prohibited from conducting political advocacy or distribution of literature on ONE premises without prior approval.

TAKE AGAIN

5. Enhance Information Security And Protection Of Assets

Chapter 2

Code of Conduct

5. Enhance Information Security And Protection Of Assets

Protecting ONE in a Connected World

Cybersecurity and data protection have become integral components of responsible business conduct and ethical governance. To safeguard our business systems and the data against growing cyberthreats, we undertake a precautionary approach to minimise risk through comprehensive cybersecurity measures.

Your security awareness is the heart of our protection

1. Safeguarding Our Assets & Information Security

ONE resources are provided to help you excel in your role. To ensure they remain available and functional, follow these three pillars.

Click on each pillar to learn more:

Click to flip
Click to flip

Use assets only for authorised business activities. Occasional, minimal personal use (like a quick personal call) is generally acceptable, but assets should never be used for outside business ventures.

Click to flip
Click to flip

Take active steps to prevent loss, damage, misuse, theft, or destruction. (e.g., Do not leave a company laptop in an unlocked car).

Click to flip
Click to flip

Use supplies and utilities mindfully to avoid waste.

2. Respecting Intellectual Property

At ONE, we strictly prohibit the infringement of intellectual property rights—whether they belong to our company, a competitor, or a third party. Infringement can lead to massive lawsuits, heavy fines, and severe reputational damage.

Prohibited actions include:

Software Piracy: Installing or using computer software or program without a valid license.

Unauthorised Copying: Reproducing manuals, t
section_5.data_privacy_tabs_excerpt:
DATA PROCESSING RESPONSIBILITY
SAFEGUARDING PERSONAL DATA
THE "NO SECRET RECORDING" RULE

In the digital age, privacy extends to our conversations and meetings. ONE maintains a strict policy regarding the capture of workplace activities.

The Golden Rule:

You must obtain prior consent from all participants before recording any meeting, call, or message.

5. Handling Confidential Information

Confidential information is any data not known to the general public that could provide a competitive advantage or cause harm if disclosed. At ONE, this includes:

Business Strategies: Future business strategies, expansion or asset plans, or pricing models.

Financial Data: Unreleased quarterly results or internal budgets.

Technical Data: Software code, proprietary algorithms, or system architecture.

Third-Party Data: Non-public information about our customers or vendors that they have entrusted to us.

Go through these scenarios below to see example in handling confidential information:

"Hey, are you heading to lunch? Are these confidential documents? Should you clear those away before you head out?"

1

It is fine, I will be back in a few minutes and we’re in a secure building.

2

You are right. I need to lock these in my drawer or take them with me.

Always remember:

You must not remove, disclose, leak, or use confidential information for any unauthorised purpose.

Your duty of con
section_5.q1_initial_excerpt:
Q.1

Scenario: Small amount

Lara just returned from a business trip. After submitting her claims, she realised she had entered the wrong amount for her hotel stay. She entered $50 more than she was billed but decided to leave it as is.

Is this acceptable?

Choose the best answer

A. Yes. The amount is too small, it would not make a difference to ONE.

B. No. By intentionally leaving the incorrect amount, Lara is engaging in deliberate misstatement and falsification.

C. No. It will cause the person who processed the claims to lose her job.

D. Yes. No one will notice it.

SUBMIT


Incorrect

Lara’s decision is a significant compliance violation. Even though $50 feels like a "small amount," it breaches the core principles of integrity and truthful reporting.

TAKE AGAIN

Q.2

Scenario: Just for a Moment

David is working on a spreadsheet that contains the names, identification numbers, salary and contact details of 500 employees. He receives an urgent text message and steps out of the office for a 10-minute break, leaving his laptop open and the spreadsheet visible so he can resume work immediately upon his return.

What is the primary risk David has created in this scenario?

Choose the best answer

A.  A violation of the "Transparency" rule.

B. A failure to safeguard personal data against unauthorised access or disclosure.

C. A violation of the "Prior Consent" rule.

D. Th
section_5.q2_initial_excerpt:
Q.2

Scenario: Just for a Moment

David is working on a spreadsheet that contains the names, identification numbers, salary and contact details of 500 employees. He receives an urgent text message and steps out of the office for a 10-minute break, leaving his laptop open and the spreadsheet visible so he can resume work immediately upon his return.

What is the primary risk David has created in this scenario?

Choose the best answer

A.  A violation of the "Transparency" rule.

B. A failure to safeguard personal data against unauthorised access or disclosure.

C. A violation of the "Prior Consent" rule.

D. There is no risk because the office is a secure environment.

SUBMIT


Incorrect

Employees are expected to safeguard personal data at all times. Leaving a screen unattended with sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) visible exposes the data to unauthorised access.

TAKE AGAIN
